title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company backed by $85M+ in funding, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale its go-to-market engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce expertise, and the ability to align Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR across globally distributed teams including the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years RevOps/GTM experience exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS fintech background aligns perfectly with Finout's cloud cost management focus, deep Salesforce expertise, and proven ability to scale beyond $30M+ ARR. Tel Aviv hybrid location is ideal for candidate profile.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations opening and thought of you immediately—your 15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS fintech is exactly what they're scaling toward. They're backed by $85M+ and need someone to orchestrate Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR across Tel Aviv and globally distributed teams, and your track record of scaling beyond $30M+ ARR and deep Salesforce expertise makes this a rare alignment. Would be worth a 15-min conversation to see if Finout's next chapter matches your next move.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a revenue intelligence platform, is hiring a RevOps Manager focused on territory strategy and operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity. Responsibilities include territory mapping in Salesforce/Fullcast, capacity modeling, and partnering with Sales, Strategy, and Finance. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, strong analytical skills in Excel, SQL, and BI tools, and proficiency with CRM systems, especially Salesforce.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years RevOps/GTM experience far exceeds 3-5yr requirement, deep Salesforce expertise, B2B SaaS domain authority, and strong analytical/forecasting background align perfectly with territory strategy and capacity modeling needs. Only minor gap is no explicit Fullcast mention, but territory management experience transfers directly.
ai_opener: Omri—I came across Gong's RevOps Manager role and it feels like a natural fit for your background. With 15+ years scaling GTM motions in B2B SaaS, you've likely tackled territory strategy and capacity modeling at scale—exactly what Gong is looking to accelerate. I'd love to explore whether this could be your next move.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pylon seeks a GTM Engineer to test, implement, and optimize AI outbound solutions, build automated outbound sequences, and own email marketing infrastructure including domain health and deliverability. You'll create data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling, collaborate with RevOps on lead scoring and routing, and ship growth experiments. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM engineering/growth roles at B2B SaaS companies, hands-on experience with marketing automation tools, and strong technical skills with APIs and webhooks.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise and technical skill set. The role directly matches his strengths in marketing automation, pipeline optimization, and data infrastructure—with the only minor gap being that this is slightly more hands-on technical/engineering-focused than his typical executive positioning.
ai_opener: Hi, I came across Pylon's GTM Engineer role and it immediately caught my attention. With 15+ years building revenue operations infrastructure at B2B SaaS companies—including deep expertise in marketing automation, API integrations, and data pipeline architecture between marketing, CRM, and sales tools—I've spent the last decade shipping exactly the kind of AI-native outbound optimization and lead scoring systems you're looking to scale. I'd love to explore whether this could be a great fit.

title: GTM Engineer

As GTM Engineer, you'll build, configure, and continuously improve these agents, working at the intersection of data, automation, and human workflows. You'll focus on how AI systems connect and run, as well as how they learn and improve over time across the company's go-to-market motion.

Only minor gap: facility management vertical is outside core fintech/proven SaaS verticals, but the AI-first GTM motion and workflow automation align perfectly with modern RevOps priorities.

The intersection of data, automation, and human workflows—especially how these systems learn and improve across Sales, Marketing, and CS—is exactly where RevOps is headed. I'd love to explore how my experience scaling GTM operations could help you build agents that actually move revenue metrics.
